articles.headline: Science finds a place on Artemis 2
articles.blurb: While the Artemis 2 mission is primarily a test flight, the four astronauts on board will conduct some science during the nearly 10-day mission.

articles.headline: Why Do Airlines Still Invest In Premium Economy Instead Of Business Class?
articles.blurb: It seems to be one of the use of space aboard aircraft for maximizing revenue.
articles.takeaway: Premium economy offers superior revenue per square foot compared to business class, making it the more efficient use of limited cabin space for airlines prioritizing yield optimization.

articles.headline: How to Assess Safety Risk
articles.blurb: A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.
articles.takeaway: Part 5 safety risk assessment frameworks enable operators to systematically quantify and mitigate hazards before they cascade into accidents.

articles.blurb: The situation caused other flights to be diverted due to the subsequent runway closure.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Organizations must document their deliberate acceptance of residual risks after implementing feasible controls, creating accountability that regulators will scrutinize during audits.

articles.headline: 7 Reasons Why The F-22 Still Outperforms Newer Jets
articles.blurb: While the F-35 now outperforms F-22 as a balanced advanced fighter, the F-22 still wins in its air dominance role and some flying characteristics.
articles.takeaway: The F-22's specialized air dominance capabilities remain superior to the more versatile F-35, suggesting role-specific design still outweighs multi-mission versatility in certain combat scenarios.

articles.takeaway: The 747's unmatched cargo volume and lower acquisition costs on the secondhand market continue to deliver better economics than modern wide-bodies for freight operators.

articles.headline: What Happens To Aircraft After Retirement?
articles.blurb: The fate of retired commercial jets is a mystery to many, but in reality, there are many interesting journeys that they can go on.
articles.takeaway: Retired aircraft generate substantial aftermarket value through conversion, leasing, and parts harvesting rather than immediate scrapping, extending asset utility across operators globally.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across all aviation segments offers immediate emissions and efficiency gains while capacity solutions are being developed to handle projected traffic growth.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: The Striking Differences Between The Airbus A321XLR & Boeing 737 MAX 10
articles.blurb: The Airbus A321XLR and Boeing 737 MAX 10 represent the pinnacle of evolution in the aviation industry, pushing narrowbodies to their absolute limits.
articles.takeaway: The A321XLR's 4,700nm range versus the 737 MAX 10's 3,550nm creates distinct operational economics for ultra-long-haul narrowbody routes.
